page: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-5760s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance featuring 24 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server operates at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz with 24 physical cores and a single thread per core configuration. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AVX, AES, SHA_NI, and BMI2 instruction sets.

The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. For storage, it bundles 5760 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity, providing substantial space for data-intensive applications. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this configuration.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong performance, placing in the top 25% for both stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests. However, users should note that the CPU allocation is shared, which may introduce performance variability under sustained, high-concurrency workloads compared to dedicated thread allocations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density of 8.0 GB per core combined with a massive 5.7 TB local NVMe SSD storage tier offers a balanced resource profile for memory-heavy and storage-bound workloads without the premium of dedicated-core instances. This configuration is suitable for large-scale databases, memory-intensive caching layers, data warehousing, and general-purpose workloads requiring substantial local storage.
description: The Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-5760s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 24 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, and includes a massive 5760 GB local NVMe SSD storage volume. In benchmark evaluations, the server delivers strong CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests. While the shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff in performance consistency under sustained peak loads, the combination of high memory capacity and extensive local NVMe storage makes this instance highly cost-efficient for resource-dense deployments. It is well-suited for large databases, caching systems, and memory-intensive data processing workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-5760s-amd features 24 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5760 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top 25% stress-ng CPU performance for memory and storage-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-5760s-amd offers 24 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5.7 TB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity memory and storage instance featuring twenty-four shared AMD EPYC cores and over five terabytes of local NVMe SSD.
bullet_points: 24 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core)
5760 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Strong top-25% single-core and multi-core stress-ng benchmark performance
Shared CPU allocation offering high memory and storage density
Optimized for large databases, caching layers, and data-intensive applications
